claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a child - my son Alex. He's 6 years old and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It sounds like you may be eligible, but I need to ask a few more questions to be sure.

Do you live in the UK, and is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Birmingham. And no, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you currently working, and if so, what is your individual annual income (or your partner's income if you have a partner)? This is important because there's a High Income Child Benefit tax charge that may apply if you or your partner earn over £60,000 per year, though it doesn't affect your eligibility to receive the benefit itself.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I do work, yes. My income is under £60,000, so I don't think that tax charge would apply to me. I'm not with a partner, so it's just my income.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarize your situation:
- You have a 6-year-old son named Alex
- Alex lives with you in the UK (Birmingham)
- You are responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- You live in the UK
- Your income is under £60,000 and you don't have a partner

Based on the Child Benefit rules, you qualify for Child Benefit because:
1. Alex is under 16 years old
2. You live in the UK
3. You are responsible for Alex (he lives with you)
4. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You should be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex!

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You live in the UK and are responsible for him
- He lives with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

Since your income is under £60,000, you won't have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge either.

You can proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit for Alex. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Birmingham), the claimant is responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. The claimant meets all these criteria for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a single parent living in the UK with their 6-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex, he lives with them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and the claimant's income is under £60,000 so no tax charge applies. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Birmingham), the claimant is responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. The claimant meets all these criteria for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a single parent living in the UK with their 6-year-old son Alex. They are responsible for Alex, he lives with them, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and the claimant's income is under £60,000 so no tax charge applies. The claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
